content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 222-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

October 12, 2018

Rebecca Moon
Office of the City Attorney
456 West Olive Avenue
Sunnyvale, CA 94088-3707

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-18-173

Dear Ms. Moon:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please note that we are only providing advice under the provisions of the Act as the Commission does not provide advice on bodies of law outside of the Act, including other laws limiting the use of public funds. In addition, please note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not the case, or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for additional advice.

QUESTION

May City staff prepare and distribute over 200 copies of a newsletter, which includes a list of candidates for City Council, given that two candidates are City Councilmembers seeking re-election?

CONCLUSION

City staff may prepare and distribute the newsletter with a list of candidates, including both Councilmembers seeking re-election, so long as the newsletter is prepared and sent completely independent of the City Council and does not "feature," or otherwise single out, either Councilmember seeking re-election.

FACTS AS PROVIDED BY THE REQUESTOR

Three times per year, the City of Sunnyvale publishes a newsletter called the "Horizon." More than 200 identical copies of the Horizon are mailed to local residents and businesses.

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-18-173
Page No. 2

In November 2018, the City will hold a City Council election. City staff intends to include
an election guide in the October 2018 issue of the Horizon, which will include general voter
information, such as: the date of the election, where to register to vote, how to locate a polling
place, and how to become an informed voter.

City staff also plans to include a list of City Council candidates whose names will appear on
the ballot. Two of the current City Councilmembers are running for re-election in 2018. Each City
Council candidate's name will appear in the same typeface, type size, and color. No information
about the candidates will be provided, except their names and the Council seat for which they are
running. The list will not include job titles, biographical information, or photographs. You indicate
that the newsletter will not be prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert
with any of the City Council members seeking re-election.

ANALYSIS

Section 89001 establishes that "no newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at public
expense." Section 89002(a) provides that mailing is prohibited by Section 89001 if all of the
following criteria are met²:

"(1) Any item sent is delivered, by any means, to the recipient at his or her
residence, place of employment or business, or post office box. The item delivered to
the recipient must be a tangible item, such as a videotape, record, or button, or a
written document.

"(2) The item sent either:

"(A) Features an elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or
sends the mailing, or

"(B) Includes the name, office, photograph, or other reference to an elected
officer affiliated with the agency which produces or sends the mailing, and is
prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with the
elected officer.

"(3)(A) Any of the costs of distribution is paid for with public money; or

"(B) Costs of design, production, and printing exceeding $50.00 are paid
with public moneys, and the design, production, or printing is done with the intent of
sending the item other than as permitted by this regulation.

"(4) More than two hundred substantially similar items are sent, in a single
calendar month, excluding any item sent in response to an unsolicited request and
any item described in subdivision (b)."

² Section 89002 contains recently codified provisions from former Regulation 18901.

File No. A-18-173
Page No. 3

Thus, a mass mailing is prohibited if all of the factors set forth in Section 89002(a) are met.
If all of the factors are not met, the mass mailing is not prohibited.

In this instance, the determinative question is whether the mailing features an elected officer
affiliated with the agency producing or sending the mailing, or whether the mailing that includes the
name, office or other reference to an elected officer is prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation,
coordination, or concert with the elected officer as provided in Section 89002(a)(2)(A)-(B).

Features an Elected Officer

A mailing "features" an elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or sends
the mailing, if the item includes the elected officer's photograph or signature, or singles out the
elected officer by the manner of display of his or her name or office in the layout of the document,
such as by headlines, captions, type size, typeface, or type color. (Section 89002(c)(2)).³

Based on the provided facts, the names of each candidate will appear in the same typeface,
font size, color, and will only include each candidate's name and the seat each candidate is running
for. Therefore, the newsletter does not "feature," or otherwise single out, either Councilmember for
purposes of Section 89002(a)(2)(A).

Sent in Cooperation or Concert with a Referenced Elected Officer

A mailing is prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with an
elected officer if the officer is in any way involved with the production or distribution of the items
sent. (Dixon Advice Letter, No. I-06-183.) For example, the elected officers affiliated with the
agency may not review the mailing or make suggestions for included material. (Stone Advice
Letter, No. A-89-670.) An item may include the names of an affiliated elected officer in a manner
that does not "feature" the officer, so long as the items are prepared and sent completely
independently of the affiliated elected officers. (Culver Advice Letter, No. A-90-140.)

Based on the facts provided, the City Council will not be involved in the review, production
or distribution of the newsletter. Therefore, the newsletter is not prepared or sent in cooperation,
consultation, coordination, or concert with an elected officer for purposes of Section
89002(a)(2)(B).

Because both of factors under Section 89002(a)(2) are not met, it is unnecessary to analyze
the remainder of Section 89002(a), as all of the elements must be met in order to be considered a
prohibited mailing. Therefore, we conclude the newsletter is not prohibited and staff may include a
list of each City Council candidate's name and the City Council seat sought.⁴

³ For purposes of the Act's mass mailing provisions, an elected officer is "affiliated" with an agency where he
or she is a member, officer, or employee of the agency, or of a subunit thereof, or has supervisory control over the
agency, or appoints one or more members of the agency. (Section 89002(c)(1).) In this case, both councilmembers fall
under the definition of elected officers who are affiliated with the City.

⁴ Because we conclude that the newsletter is not a prohibited mass mailing under Section 89002(a), we do not
further analyze whether the list of candidates qualifies as a "roster," for purposes of Sections 89002(b)(1) and 89003.

File No. A-18-173
Page No. 4

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Dave Bainbridge
General Counsel

By: Sara K. Puricelli
Counsel, Legal Division

SKP:gal

May City staff prepare and distribute over 200 copies of a newsletter, which includes a list 
of candidates for City Council, given that two candidates are City Councilmembers seeking re-
election?
sections.conclusion:
City staff may prepare and distribute the newsletter with a list of candidates, including both 
Councilmembers seeking re-election, so long as the newsletter is prepared and sent completely 
independent of the City Council and does not "feature," or otherwise single out, either 
Councilmember seeking re-election. 
FACTS AS PROVIDED BY THE REQUESTOR 
Three times per year, the City of Sunnyvale publishes a newsletter called the "Horizon." 
More than 200 identical copies of the Horizon are mailed to local residents and businesses.In November 2018, the City will hold a City Council election. City staff intends to include 
an election guide in the October 2018 issue of the Horizon, which will include general voter 
information, such as: the date of the election, where to register to vote, how to locate a polling 
place, and how to become an informed voter. 
City staff also plans to include a list of City Council candidates whose names will appear on 
the ballot. Two of the current City Councilmembers are running for re-election in 2018. Each City

running. The list will not include job titles, biographical information, or photographs. You indicate 
that the newsletter will not be prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert 
with any of the City Council members seeking re-election.
sections.analysis:
Section 89001 establishes that "no newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at public 
expense." Section 89002(a) provides that mailing is prohibited by Section 89001 if all of the 
following criteria are met2: 
"(1) Any item sent is delivered, by any means, to the recipient at his or her 
residence, place of employment or business, or post office box. The item delivered to 
the recipient must be a tangible item, such as a videotape, record, or button, or a 
written document. 
"(2) The item sent either: 
"(A) Features an elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or 
sends the mailing, or 
"(B) Includes the name, office, photograph, or other reference to an elected 
officer affiliated with the agency which produces or sends the mailing, and is 
prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with the 
elected officer. 
"(3)(A) Any of the costs of distribution is paid for with public money; or 
"(B) Costs of design, production, and printing exceeding $50.00 are paid 
with public moneys, and the design, production, or printing is done with the intent of 
sending the item other than as permitted by this regulation. 
"(4) More than two hundred substantially similar items are sent, in a single 
calendar month, excluding any item sent in response to an unsolicited request and 
any item described in subdivision (b)." 
2 Section 89002 contains recently codified provisions from former Regulation 18901 .  
Thus, a mass mailing is prohibited if all of the factors set forth in Section 89002(a) are met. 
If all of the factors are not met, the mass mailing is not prohibited. 
In this instance, the determinative question is whether the mailing features an elected officer 
affiliated with the agency producing or sending the mailing, or whether the mailing that includes the 
name, office or other reference to an elected officer is prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, 
coordination, or concert with the elected officer as provided in Section 89002(a)(2)(A)-(B). 
Features an Elected Officer 
A mailing "features" an elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or sends 
the mailing, if the item includes the elected officer's photograph or signature, or singles out the 
elected officer by the manner of display of his or her name or office in the layout of the document, 
such as by headlines, captions, type size, typeface, or type color. (Section 89002(c)(2).)3 
Based on the provided facts, the names of each candidate will appear in the same typeface, 
font size, color, and will only include each candidate's name and the seat each candidate is running 
for. Therefore, the newsletter does not "feature," or otherwise single out, either Councilmember for 
purposes of Section 89002(a)(2)(A). 
Sent in Cooperation or Concert with a Referenced Elected Officer 
A mailing is prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with an 
elected officer if the officer is in any way involved with the production or distribution of the items 
sent. (Dixon Advice Letter, No. I-06-183.) For example, the elected officers affiliated with the 
agency may not review the mailing or make suggestions for included material. (Stone Advice 
Letter, No. A-89-670.) An item may include the names of an affiliated elected officer in a manner 
that does not "feature" the officer, so long as the items are prepared and sent completely 
independently of the affiliated elected officers. (Culver Advice Letter, No. A-90-140.) 
Based on the facts provided, the City Council will not be involved in the review, production 
or distribution of the newsletter. Therefore, the newsletter is not prepared or sent in cooperation, 
consultation, coordination, or concert with an elected officer for purposes of Section 
89002( a)(2)(B). 
Because both of factors under Section 89002(a)(2) are not met, it is unnecessary to analyze 
the remainder of Section 89002(a), as all of the elements must be met in order to be considered a 
prohibited mailing. Therefore, we conclude the newsletter is not prohibited and staff may include a 
list of each City Council candidate's name and the City Council seat sought.4 
3 For purposes of the Act's mass mailing provisions, an elected officer is "affiliated" with an agency where he 
or she is a member, officer, or employee of the agency, or of a subunit thereof, or has supervisory control over the 
agency, or appoints one or more members of the agency. (Section 89002(c)(l).) In this case, both councilmembers fall 
under the definition of elected officers who are affiliated with the City. 
4 Bee:ause we conclude that the newsletter is not a prohibited mass mailing under Section 89002(a), we do not 
further analyze whether the list of candidates qualifies as a "roster," for purposes of Sections 89002(b )( l) and 89003.
